title: Urceolina tenera
taxonomy: Urceolina tenera is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Amaryllidaceae, native to South America. It belongs to the genus Urceolina, which is a small group of plants that are closely related to the genus Crinum. The genus Urceolina is further divided into several species, with Urceolina tenera being one of them. This classification is based on the plant's physical characteristics, such as its flowers and leaves, as well as its genetic makeup. The Amaryllidaceae family is a large and diverse group of plants that includes many species of bulbs and flowering plants.

intro: Urceolina tenera, also known as the "Yellow Onion Weed" or "Hemerocallis fulva", is a plant species belonging to the lily family (Hemerocallidaceae) that grows in tropical and subtropical regions.
